frameworks.name: recurrent neural network (RNN)
frameworks.one_line: RNN whose temporal dynamics are aligned to human reaction times

frameworks.name: Wong-Wang decision-making model
frameworks.one_line: popular attractor decision model re-implemented as a trainable deep network

frameworks.name: ideal observer
frameworks.one_line: speed-accuracy optimal RNN trained without human RT data

frameworks.name: convolutional neural network (CNN)
frameworks.one_line: feedforward visual front-end fed into the decision RNN

core_question: Can the number of recurrent time steps an RNN takes to decide be aligned to human reaction-time distributions?
key_assumptions: perceptual decisions have rich dynamic structure beyond mean accuracy
an RNN's iteration count can serve as a proxy for decision latency
a differentiable approximation to step-count allows gradient training against RTs
manipulations: training signal: human RTs vs. no human data (ideal-observer objective)
stimulus type: artificial vs. natural images
decision architecture: generic RNN vs. Wong-Wang implementation
key_findings: the approximation constrains RNN step counts to match human RTs across psychophysics tasks
an ideal-observer RNN trained for speed-accuracy tradeoff also fits human RTs
a deep Wong-Wang model coupled with a CNN accounts for behaviour on natural images
interpretations: RT-aligned RNNs unify dynamic decision models with deep vision models
speed-accuracy optimisation alone yields humanlike temporal behaviour
